title: The hedge that hid 3,000 ancient silver coins
description: The discovery was made by farm labourers clearing a hedge in 1866.

title: The Strait of Hormuz Proves Fossil Fuels are Essential for Food Security
description: For Years, climax campaigners have claimed that our food supply is under grave threat from climate change caused by excessive fossil fuel use. Ironically, the war in the Middle East is highlighting that the much bigger food challenge for the world is not having enough access to fossil fuels. Today, half of all the calories [...] The post The Strait of Hormuz Proves Fossil Fuels are Essential for Food Security appeared first on The Namibian .

title: Towers Of Silence Go Viral: Ancient Funeral Ritual Sparks Disease Spread Fears And Heated Debate
description: The Towers of Silence, an ancient Zoroastrian funerary practice, have gone viral, sparking global debate over cultural traditions, public health concerns, and the impact of social media on sacred rituals.

title: Pupils off to Paris and ancient Rome in nostalgic look back gallery
description: We get nostalgic as we take another look back at some moments from the Chester Standard archives.

title: In 1973, a thin wooden scrap from a Roman fort revealed Britain’s most personal ancient letter
description: Archaeologists unearthed fragile wooden tablets at Vindolanda, a Roman fort in England, revealing intimate details of daily life beyond military might. These 'trash' fragments, preserved in waterlogged soil, offer personal correspondence and administrative lists, proving that even ordinary objects can rewrite history and highlight the crucial role of observation in uncovering the past.
